iterate_latest.summary: Markdown editor round-trip now preserves YAML frontmatter, line endings (CRLF) and the trailing newline via a verbatim envelope; the diff shows only real body changes and Save no longer corrupts frontmatter.

surface_verification.evidence: 4/4 passed in Chromium against an isolated Vite (BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5199); API route-mocked

behaviors.behavior: AC1 unedited canonical frontmatter file round-trips byte-identically
behaviors.evidence: markdownTiptap.envelope.test.ts AC1 PASSED + E2E 'No changes' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC2 a single body edit yields exactly a one-line (+1/-1) diff
behaviors.evidence: markdownTiptap.envelope.test.ts AC2 PASSED + E2E add=1/del=1 PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC3 YAML frontmatter preserved byte-for-byte (no setext-heading mangling)
behaviors.evidence: markdownTiptap.envelope.test.ts AC3 PASSED + E2E PUT-body verbatim frontmatter PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC4 CRLF line endings preserved across round-trip
AC5 trailing-newline convention preserved (with and without)
behaviors.evidence: markdownTiptap.envelope.test.ts AC5 PASSED + E2E save endsWith newline PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC7 detectLossyConstructs no longer flags YAML frontmatter
Modal shows neutral 'frontmatter preserved' note, not a lossy warn
Save is a disabled no-op when the round-trip is byte-identical
behaviors.evidence: E2E 'No changes' (Save disabled) + modal Save tests require a real edit PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Leading indentation of first body line stays in core, not prefix (ext-review #1)
Save writes byte-correct bytes (frontmatter verbatim, edit present, no heading)
